21.correct: On Vaisakhi in 1699, Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa Panth at Anandpur Sahib, initiating the Panj Pyare.

wrong.A: Guru Nanak Dev was born in 1469, not on this Vaisakhi.
wrong.B: The founding of the Khalsa, not the first Sikh temple, is the event.
wrong.D: The revelation of the Quran is unrelated to Vaisakhi 1699.

21.fact: The Khalsa introduced the five Ks and the names 'Singh' and 'Kaur'.

22.correct: Lying down spreads your weight over a larger area, so the pressure on the sand is less (pressure = force / area).

wrong.A: Pressure becomes smaller, not bigger, over a larger area.
wrong.C: The pressure is reduced, not merely 'uniform'.
wrong.D: The pressure decreases but is not zero.

22.fact: This is why lying flat helps distribute weight on soft or unstable surfaces.

23.correct: The Meghalaya Plateau is a detached extension of the Peninsular Plateau of India.

wrong.A: It is not part of the Western Ghats.
wrong.B: It is a part of the broader peninsular block, not specifically the Deccan Plateau.
wrong.D: It is not part of the Chotanagpur Plateau, though both are peninsular.

23.fact: The Meghalaya Plateau (Shillong Plateau) got separated from the main peninsula by the Garo-Rajmahal gap.

24.correct: The 1991 reforms are sometimes called 'jobless growth' because they produced high GDP growth without a proportional expansion of employment.

wrong.A: Agricultural employment did not rise sharply.
wrong.C: Educated unemployment did not fall rapidly.
wrong.D: Rural areas did not absorb much more labour.

24.fact: Services-led growth created relatively fewer jobs than the size of the workforce required.

25.correct: Chabahar Port is strategically located at the crossroads of South Asia, Central Asia and the Middle East.

wrong.A: It does not connect East Asia and Africa primarily.
wrong.C: It is not at the Europe-East Asia junction.
wrong.D: South America is not part of its strategic location.

25.fact: Chabahar gives India sea-land access to Afghanistan and Central Asia, bypassing Pakistan.

26.correct: The New Industrial Policy of 1980 aimed to encourage modernization of small-scale industry and the absorption of improved technology.

wrong.B: It did not aim to close private industries.
wrong.C: It did not aim at stricter control over the public sector.
wrong.D: It did not aim to nationalize major industries.

26.fact: The 1980 policy laid groundwork for the gradual liberalization that accelerated in 1991.

27.correct: In a mixed economy, setting the prices of all goods is NOT a function of the government - prices are largely determined by market forces.

wrong.B: Regulating markets is a government function.
wrong.C: Providing public goods is a government function.
wrong.D: Redistributing income (through taxes/transfers) is a government function.

27.fact: A mixed economy combines private markets with government intervention, as in India.

28.correct: Cotton cultivation has the strongest agro-ecological link with the black soil (regur) basins of India, as black soil retains moisture well.

wrong.A: Jute grows in alluvial deltaic soils, not black soil.
wrong.C: Tea grows in acidic hill soils.

28.fact: Black soil (regur) is also called 'black cotton soil' because of its suitability for cotton.

29.correct: The 'Nyay Shruti' app was launched in August 2024 by the Ministry of Home Affairs.

wrong.A: It was not launched by the Ministry of Law and Justice.
wrong.B: It was not launched by the Ministry of Defence.
wrong.D: It was not launched by the Ministry of Communications.

29.fact: Such apps support the rollout of the new criminal laws and justice-delivery reforms.

30.correct: In November 2024 the Supreme Court of India held that a sexual-harassment case cannot be closed merely on a compromise between the parties, given the serious social impact of such offences.

wrong.B: This ruling is attributed to the Supreme Court, not the Patna High Court.
wrong.C: It was not the Madras High Court.
wrong.D: It was not a Delhi family court.

30.fact: Crimes against women are treated as offences against society, not just private disputes.

31.correct: The Chalukya ruler Pulakeshin II defeated Harshavardhana on the banks of the Narmada river.

wrong.A: Rajendra I was a Chola king.
wrong.C: Krishna III was a Rashtrakuta king.
wrong.D: 'Rajasimha' is not the ruler who defeated Harsha here.

31.fact: This victory halted Harsha's southward expansion and is recorded in the Aihole inscription.

32.correct: Uttarakhand is known for the 'Choliya' folk dance, a sword dance performed at weddings and festivals.

wrong.A: Kolkali is a folk art of Kerala.
wrong.B: This option does not name an Uttarakhand dance.

32.fact: The Choliya dance has martial roots, depicting the valour of Rajput warriors.

33.correct: In 2025 Indian Railways successfully tested the country's first hydrogen-powered coach at Chennai (Integral Coach Factory).

wrong.B: Rae Bareli was not the test location cited.
wrong.C: Kapurthala was not where it was tested.

33.fact: Hydrogen trains emit only water vapour, supporting green transport goals.

34.correct: As per the 2011 Census, Uttar Pradesh recorded the maximum rural population of any Indian state.

wrong.A: Bihar has a large rural population but less than UP.
wrong.C: Maharashtra has a large urban share.
wrong.D: West Bengal has fewer rural people than UP.

34.fact: Uttar Pradesh is also India's most populous state overall.

35.correct: Satara was annexed in 1848 under Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse.
35.fact: The Doctrine of Lapse annexed states whose rulers died without a natural heir, including Jhansi and Nagpur.

36.correct: Royal Challengers Bengaluru won the Women's Premier League (WPL) title in 2024.

wrong.A: In 2023 (the inaugural WPL) Mumbai Indians won.

36.fact: The WPL is the women's franchise T20 league launched by the BCCI in 2023.

37.correct: The 2030 Asian Games will be held in Doha, Qatar.
37.fact: The 2026 Asian Games are scheduled for Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, and 2034 for Riyadh.

38.correct: 'Pommel horse' and 'parallel bars' are apparatus/terms associated with gymnastics.
38.fact: Men's artistic gymnastics includes six apparatus, including the pommel horse and parallel bars.

39.correct: UNESCO publishes the Global Education Monitoring (GEM) Report.

wrong.B: The UNDP publishes the Human Development Report.
wrong.C: The World Bank does not publish the GEM Report.

39.fact: The GEM Report tracks progress towards global education goals (SDG 4).

40.correct: The Urban Governance Index (UGI) 2024 evaluated Indian states across 4 main categories.

wrong.A: 2 is fewer than the categories used.
wrong.C: 6 is not the number of categories.

40.fact: Such governance indices help benchmark the performance of urban local bodies.
